mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2%
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 103.0 (index 2017=100) signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 103.0 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, which triggers equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points in a single month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock event
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.4%) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic confidence, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST remains above 1,200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust aggregate demand and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
